extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won from a reduced peloton sprint after late crash took down lead-out trains with 250m remaining
tactical_patterns: Trentin exploited Bora-Hansgrohe's leadout with only two riders, sitting on their train until 200m marker rather than organizing own.
Dillier swept all four KOM climbs from breakaway specifically to secure classification, showing targeted classification hunting over stage ambitions.
Sky controlled 212km stage despite wet conditions to protect slim 9-second GC lead, indicating defensive posture prevailed over attacking.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: Successfully timed sprint opening just before 200m after parasiting larger team's leadout, showing tactical patience from two-rider team
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Bora-Hansgrohe deployed full leadout train but lost stage despite numerical advantage, suggesting poor final positioning or timing
rider_signals.DILLIER Sylvan: positive: Methodically collected points on all four climbs from breakaway rather than contesting stage, demonstrating classification-focused racing discipline
rider_signals.MOSKUN Johnny: neutral: Sky controlled pace defensively for 212km wet stage to protect 9 seconds, prioritizing GC defense over stage hunting
